title: Worst-case key-comparison count of insertion sort (WHILE / imperative)
statement_informal: Sorting a list of length n by in-place insertion sort, written in a WHILE language, performs at most n(n-1)/2 key comparisons.

cost_model.notes: Big-step cost-annotated semantics of a small WHILE language; only key comparisons (the BKLe form, array element vs. held key) are counted. Index/bookkeeping tests (BLe) are free.

references: Neil D. Jones, Computability and Complexity from a Programming Perspective — WHILE as the canonical language for complexity
Nielson & Nielson, Semantics with Applications — cost-annotated operational semantics
